name: action-sheet
description: Bottom-rising sheet of discrete actions (iOS action-sheet pattern) with a Cancel button

meta.name: ActionSheet
meta.description: Bottom-rising sheet of discrete actions (iOS action-sheet pattern) with a Cancel button

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Whether the component is open on first render (uncontrolled).

props.name: onOpenChange
props.description: Called with the next open state when it changes.

props.name: actions
props.description: Choices, each with a label, onSelect, and optional destructive/disabled flags

props.name: showCancel
props.description: When true, shows a cancel button below the actions.

props.name: labels
props.description: Overrides for the component’s user-visible strings (i18n).

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Basic
examples.code:
<ActionSheet
  open={isOpen}
  onOpenChange={setIsOpen}
  title="Share photo"
  actions={[
    { label: 'Copy link', onSelect: copyLink },
    { label: 'Delete', onSelect: remove, destructive: true },
  ]}
/>

examples.title: Without cancel button
examples.code: <ActionSheet defaultOpen showCancel={false} actions={[{ label: "Archive", onSelect: archive }]} />
examples.description: Escape and outside press still dismiss the sheet.

whenToUse: A short list of discrete actions on a touch surface, rising from the bottom
Confirming or choosing among a few operations (e.g. Share, Edit, Delete) on mobile
A mobile-first alternative to an anchored dropdown menu when there is no trigger anchor
whenNotToUse: A single yes/no confirmation — use AlertDialog
A form or scrollable content — use BottomSheet or Sheet
A menu anchored to a trigger on desktop — use Menu or Dropdown

antiPatterns.why: A one-action sheet is a confirmation; AlertDialog states the decision clearly

related.name: BottomSheet
related.reason: Use BottomSheet for rich/resizable content; ActionSheet is a fixed list of actions

related.name: Menu
related.reason: Use Menu for a trigger-anchored dropdown on pointer-first surfaces

intent.a11yRationale: Renders role="menu" with role="menuitem" buttons under vertical roving focus (Arrow keys, Home/End, wrapping). The title labels the menu via aria-labelledby (or a built-in label otherwise) and the description via aria-describedby. FocusScope traps and restores focus; DismissableLayer handles Escape and outside-pointer dismissal; a separate Cancel button provides an explicit non-destructive exit.

flexibility.note: Any number of actions; each may be destructive or disabled
showCancel toggles the separate Cancel button (Escape/outside press still dismiss)
